entry.title: Re: Problem reinstalling SAP ASE
entry.content: I should have added this but you can uninstall with flags as well: uninstall.exe -i silent -DUNINSTALL_DELETE_DATA_FILES=TRUE -DUNINSTALL_DEL_FILE_SCOPE_1=DataAccess -DUNINSTALL_DEL_FILE_SCOPE_2=DataAccess64 -DUNINSTALL_DEL_FILE_SCOPE_3=OCS-16_0

entry.title: Re: Native Promotion Engine Crashes Due to Integer Overfl...
entry.content: Hello @balaji_k1, Thank you for your detailed answer. I don't understand how you could not reproduce the overflow locally using the OOTB data model since the issue is not related to the product but to the quantity purchased (AbstractOrderEntryModel.quantity which is of type Long). Regarding the volume of purchased products in Production : 1/ we do confirm that our B2B clients have purchased 17,500,000,000 mm² of tole sheets (see picture attached) 2/ we cannot use m² instead of mm² because the conversion would result in floating numbers. For example, converting 4,397,700,000 mm² to m² would give 4 397,7 m² that cannot be stored in AbstractOrderEntryModel.quantity (see also picture attached). Indeed, changing the data type for order entries quantities in promotion engine from int to long was hectic and not native friendly and would prevent us from moving to cloud since customizations of native modules are no longer tolerated that's why we are asking the solution team to come up with a more elegant implementation of order data population

entry.title: Re: Payment rejected by the bank - Amount Wrong - Structu...
entry.content:
When supplier invoices and credit notes are included together in the same payment run, SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud generates a payment file that contains separate line items for each document, including both positive (invoices) and negative (credit notes) amounts. Although SAP correctly calculates and sends the final net payable amount, the payment file still lists the individual document amounts at item level.

During the bank’s internal business validation, the payment is rejected with the message “wrong amount”, because the bank expects one single net payment amount per supplier, without mixed positive and negative line items. When multiple items with different signs are present for the same supplier, the bank’s validation logic rejects the payment even though the total amount is correct.

SAP does not net invoices and credit notes into a single line at the payment-file level. For this reason, the recommended best approach is to clear credit notes against invoices before running the payment. Once the items are cleared, only the net open balance remains, and the generated payment file contains a single payable amount per supplier, which the bank accept.

Clearing means offsetting the invoice and the credit note inside SAP so that both documents are closed. After clearing, only the net open balance (if any) remains to be paid. When the payment is then created, SAP generates a file with one single payable amount, which aligns with the bank’s validation rules and is accepted without issues.

entry.title: Re: How to integrate SAP S/4HANA Cloud with Third-Party Payroll Provider?

entry.title: Re: Collision Handling between Absences and Attendances in SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking - 1H 202
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for the insightful blog.

I would like to ask whether collision handling between Attendance and Attendance is also supported.

We have a requirement where an external time clock (WORK time type) should be allowed to overlap with certain Attendance types (e.g., half day Training). However, overlaps between specific Attendance types—such as Training and Work Outside—should not be permitted.

Is it possible to support this type of scenario?

Thank you in advance for your clarification.

entry.title: Re: Integrating Google Gemini AI with SAPUI5 Frontend using Node.js Backend
entry.content: @RACHID_AITBOUALE Thank you for your valuable feedback and excellent suggestion! Yes, using SAP BTP Destinations for secure API key management would be the ideal production-grade approach, and I’m planning to explore that in future enhancements.

entry.title: Re: Run Statutory Reports - Post Tax Payable
entry.content:
Hello!

The G/L account suggested  (for example, 470*) is taken from the tax account determination assigned to the tax codes, and not from whether the final VAT position is payable or receivable. In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, the system does not automatically switch between 470* (VAT receivable) and 475* (VAT payable) based on the result of the VAT return.

There is no specific configuration within Model 303 to define different G/L accounts for payable and receivable scenarios.

If a different account (such as 475*) is required from an accounting perspective, this would need to be handled through tax account determination or manual adjustment at posting.

entry.title: Re: Deployment Error In SAP Build Process Automation // D...
entry.content: The issue should be resolved now after they have scaled the database and the backlog should now be cleared.

entry.title: Re: Eligible Users with Multiple Employments not appearin...
entry.content: @RyanPerkins I was able through the support ticket to look at things in detail and can't find anything wrong for the moment. So far I think it's data related, for example when you look up that employee userID that has both jobs (the inactive and the active one - 725892), we land in EC on the inactive one who is ineligible for the template. And I think there is a conflict there with the hierarchy.

entry.title: Next-level Business Transformation at Molex with Tony Gainsford
entry.content:
Next-level Business Transformation with Tony Gainsford

Digital transformation in supply chain is often discussed but rarely executed well. In a recent episode of the Future of Procurement podcast, Tony Gainsford, Business Transformation Leader for Supply Chain at Molex, shared how a global manufacturing leader moved from fragmented, analog supplier interactions to a connected, real-time supply chain. 🌍

Tony’s story offers practical lessons for any organization struggling to modernize supplier collaboration while balancing technology, people, and process discipline.

Why Supplier Collaboration Became a Strategic Imperative 🔗

Molex manufactures connectors used in smartphones, vehicles, data centers, and industrial equipment. With global scale comes operational complexity. Traditional ERP systems work well inside company boundaries, but once a purchase order leaves the enterprise, processes often degrade into emails, spreadsheets, or even fax machines. 📠

At the same time, customer expectations have accelerated dramatically. What once took days now takes hours. To meet delivery commitments and protect customer experience, Molex needed real-time visibility, faster communication, and tighter coordination with suppliers.

Building Capability, Not Just Technology 🧠

A defining factor in Molex’s success was its focus on building long-term capabilities rather than executing a one-time system rollout. Molex invested in a dedicated supplier onboarding and enablement function, staffed with people whose sole responsibility was helping suppliers adopt new ways of working. 🤝

Instead of issuing mandates or sending mass emails, Molex emphasized human connection. Every supplier was contacted personally, expectations were clearly explained, and feedback was actively collected. Training was delivered in modern, bite-sized formats such as short videos rather than dense manuals. 🎥

Measuring What Matters: KPIs and Discipline 📊

Transformation without measurement quickly turns into guesswork. From day one, Molex defined clear KPIs, including order confirmation within 48 hours. These metrics were monitored in real time, enabling teams to intervene quickly when problems emerged. 🚦

This rigor resulted in a 93%+ order confirmation rate, dramatically improving predictability across the supply chain. Buyers could focus on exceptions rather than chasing every order, and suppliers had a clear understanding of performance expectations. 🎯

Unlocking Insight Through Process and Data Mining 🔍

Molex also leveraged process mining and data analytics to uncover hidden inefficiencies. By defining a “happy path” and continuously monitoring deviations, the team quickly identified breakdowns across requisitioning, approvals, and supplier communications.

In one instance, process mining revealed buyers were still exporting purchase orders to PDF and emailing them to suppliers—despite having a live digital network. These insights allowed Molex to retrain users, clarify expectations, and refine system configurations. 📄➡️📧

Redefining the Role of the Buyer 🤖➡️🧑‍💼

As automation increased, the buyer’s role evolved. Manual confirmations, low-value approvals, and repetitive data checks were reduced or eliminated. Buyers were freed to focus on higher-value activities such as supplier relationships, risk management, and strategic problem-solving. 💡

Trust in automation came from correct system setup and strong training. When master data, business rules, and tolerances were configured properly, buyers gained confidence in letting systems run with minimal intervention. 🛠️

Looking Ahead: Speed, Intelligence, and Human Connection 🔮

Looking forward, Tony emphasized velocity as the defining characteristic of modern procurement. Processes will feel increasingly consumer-like—real-time, intuitive, and data-driven. AI and intelligent agents will handle much of the tactical work, enabling procurement teams to focus on insight and strategy. ⚡

Yet human relationships will remain essential. Automation can accelerate execution, but trust, collaboration, and partnership will continue to differentiate high-performing procurement organizations. ❤️

Final Thought: Start With a Vision ✨

Tony’s closing advice was clear: do not begin a transformation without a well-defined vision. Organizations must know what “good” looks like, align leadership early, and invest in people as much as platforms. When suppliers, processes, and insights are connected, procurement stops reacting and starts leading.

 

Apple:

https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/episode-030-next-level-business-transformation-with/id1764078646?i=1000764005688

Spotify:

https://open.spotify.com/episode/3QD8OcgVvph7DIQN0cZXdA?si=UFq7PVSqSx6VHPDnZfo-0A

entry.title: How to Configure Alert/Notification from BizX To‑Do for E‑Learning Courses?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I would like to understand how alert or notification functionality can be configured from BizX To‑Do tasks for e‑learning courses in SAP SuccessFactors Learning.

Requirement:

  • When an e‑learning course is assigned to a user, a To‑Do task in BizX should be created.
  • The user should receive a notification/alert (email or system notification) prompting them to complete the assigned course.
  • Ideally, reminders should also be triggered based on due dates (for example, upcoming due date or overdue status).

Questions:

  1. Is it possible to create or synchronize BizX To‑Do tasks based on Learning course assignments?
  2. Which configuration areas are involved (for example, Learning notifications, Intelligent Services, or BizX integrations)?
  3. Are there any standard KBA, guides, or best practices for this setup?
  4. Any known limitations or prerequisites that should be considered?

I would really appreciate guidance or examples from anyone who has configured this successfully.

Thank you in advance for your support!

Best regards,
Jyoti

entry.title: Re: Chart of Accounts in Parallel Line - Test environemen...
entry.content:
Dear @Killmonger,

In Public Cloud this should be treated as a Parallel Line lifecycle / transport alignment topic, not as a Chart of Accounts functional issue

A branch copies the current main-line configuration into the parallel line and provisions the additional Customizing tenant D.120 and Test tenant T.120. However this does not mean that the original transport requests from the main-line customizing tenant are copied as identical TRs into D.120. SAP manages the parallel line through the official Branch / Rebase / Merge lifecycle

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/configuring-sap-s4hana-cloud-public-edition-with-sap-central-business-configuration/understanding-the-parallel-line-capability

For SIT in T.120, please check:

  1. Whether the Chart of Accounts changes were already included in the branch baseline
  2. Whether a Rebase is required because the changes were made in the main line after the branch
  3. Whether the related staging entries were activated in Display Business Configuration Stagings after rebase
  4. Whether any affected configuration activity is excluded from branch/rebase and therefore requires double maintenance in the parallel line
  5. Whether the relevant D.120 ->T.120 import step was completed

SAP also confirms that there is no direct transport path between T.100 and T.120 so the alignment must be done through the parallel-line process, not by synchronizing the two test tenants directly

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3658337

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3739049

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003668525

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Chart of Accounts in Parallel Line - Test environemen...
entry.content: good

entry.title: Dimension Hierarchy Text gets truncated in SAC stories
entry.content:
Hello,

We have a SAC story that uses dimension hierarchies in widgets. The text of the hierarchy node in those widgets is getting truncated. In the SAP BW, the hierarchy is maintained correctly. But when used in SAC stories, it gets truncated.  The size of the widget does not impact; even with larger widgets, the text does not display correctly. 

Please let me know how we can resolve this.

Regards,

Pratik Shah

entry.title: Re: Issue on updating Catalog Profile on Function Location
entry.content:
Suggested approach to isolate the issue (step‑by‑step):

1) Verify Catalog Profile in standard SAP (IL02 / IL03)

  • Check whether the Catalog Profile is displayed correctly in IL02 and IL03 under Organization → Responsibilities (or if customized screen layout, then relevant tab where it was added).
  • If the value is missing, different, or inconsistent between IL02 and IL03, this strongly points to a buffer inconsistency.
  • In that case, coordinate with SAP Basis to refresh application server buffers or clear table buffers using standard repair reports.

2) Validate Data Origin settings (Inheritance vs Individual)

  • Go to IL02 → Edit → Data Origin → Data Origin List → Catalog Profile.
  • Check the Origin Field value (e.g., Ref. Functional Location, Functional Location, or Individual).
  • After updating via FM/BAPI, the origin must be set to “Individual”.
  • If inheritance is not overridden:
    • Confirm that a COMMIT is being executed.
    • Check the change log / action log for the Functional Location to verify updates are saved.
    • Ensure BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT is called explicitly after BAPI_FUNCLOC_CHANGE.

3) Final check – BAPI update indicator (X‑structure)

  • Before calling BAPI_FUNCLOC_CHANGE, verify that in DATA_GENERALX, the field CATPROFILE = 'X' is set.
  • If this indicator is not set, SAP will ignore the update, even if the value is passed correctly in the data structure.

Hope this is Helpful !

entry.title: Re: CMS components extending SimpleCMSComponent are showi...
entry.content:
Hello Harish!

This behavior is caused by the SimpleCMSComponent being on the blocked list of SmartEdit components. More information about them can be found in SAP Commerce Cloud - Managing Components. 

In order to resolve it, kindly follow these steps:

  1. Verify whether the storefront implementation or version supports the blocked component.
  2. If supported, remove the component from the blocklist attribute.
  3. Edit the component in SmartEdit as required.

Kind Regards,
Wesley
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Sneak Peek in to SAP Analytics Cloud release for Q2 2026
entry.content:
@Orla_C The Asymmetric reporting is available in SAC Excel Add-in or SAC story as the introduction talks about about the asymmetric block filter in Excel Add-in but the screenshot is of SAC story and demo video is again of SAC Excel. Kindly clarify.

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: SAP Commerce Cloud CCv2 endpoint without path for mul...
entry.content:
Hello there!

You can configure that in the manifest.json, by adding the extensions under the desired the aspect and configuring its "contextPath" as you need like in the example of the screenshot in Storefront Endpoint - Build Manifest Storefront Endpoint Configuration. You can leave their contextPath empty and then linking them to a specific endpoint and webroot. 

Kind Regards,
Wesley
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: For the PR Header text Mandatory when PR select prior...
entry.content:
Dear @Joy14,

In Public Cloud this requirement is not covered by standard configuration/SSCUI. The suitable clean-core approach is to implement a validation using the released Cloud Badi MM_PUR_S4_PR_CHECK Check of Purchase Requisition Item. This Badi also working for Header level

This Badi is intended to validate standard and extension fields and can return error messages that block the PR save. I have prepared a ready-to-use code pattern, which only needs to be adjusted with your-specific field used for the required text/justification. However based on the available PR header fields in the Badi interface the standard PR Header Text field is not exposed there. The complete list of available header and item fields can be checked directly in the Badi documentation/interface in the Custom Logic app

Example logic pattern:

LOOP AT purchaserequisitionitem_table INTO DATA(ls_item).

  IF ls_item-YOUR_CUSTOM_FIELD_ITEM_LEVEL = 'HIGH'

     AND YOUR_SECOND_CONDITION = YOUR_CONDITION.

    APPEND VALUE #(

      messagetype        = 'E'

      messageid          = '00'

      messagenumber      = '398'

      messagevariable1   = 'One of the PR items has Priority HIGH.'

      messagevariable2   = 'YOUR FIELD is required.'

      documentitemnumber = ls_item-purchaserequisitionitem

    ) TO messages.

    purchaserequisitionhaserror = abap_true.

    RETURN.

  ENDIF.

ENDLOOP.

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3268822

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3643255

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-s4hanacloud-procurement-purchasereq-checks..html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/003174573d144755b4899fad743c45aa.html

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: How to Redirect SAP CC Backoffice to Custom Domain
entry.content:
Hey there!

This is achievable by following the config steps mentioned in Configuring Extension Web Paths in Aspects and Setting the Webroot Path of an Extension! Let me know if you don't have access to these Help Portal pages so I can share with you them.

Kind Regards,
Wesley
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Week 4: AI Developer Challenge SAP-RPT-1 Model
entry.content: Week 4 challenge complete - user id GE312516

entry.title: Re: How to add a check for requisitioner for purchase req...
entry.content:
This is possible using the WORKORDER_UPDATE – BEFORE_UPDATE BAdI implementation which can be used to perform custom validations immediately when the user clicks 'Save' before the work order data is committed.

In this case, a custom check for updates to the AFNAM field (Name of Requisitioner/Requester) can be implemented by:

  • For Operation by comparing before-update data from IT_OPERATION_OLD_AFVC with after-update data from IT_OPERATION at the operation level.
  • For Component we cannot compare before-update data from IT_COMPONENT_OLD with after-update data from IT_COMPONENT at the component level as IT_COMPONENT_OLD does not contain the AFNAM field by default. Alternate approach is to retrieve the PR number from RESB and then derive AFNAM from the EBAN table. Or bit complex but future extension possibility AFNAM can be populated explicitly within the BAdI implementation. Reference: Solved: Change BADI Interface - SAP Community

entry.title: Requesting an Onboarding Advisor for my Cloud ERP Private customer
entry.content: I am a Partner CEE.  How do I request an onboarding advisor be assigned to my ccflex Adopt & Operate customer who bought Cloud ERP Private?

entry.title: CBC - new Plant is not deployed
entry.content:
I created and confirmed a new Plant but it was never deployed.

The new one but it isn't being transferred to the Customizing system, and no transport is being generated either.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: CBC - new Plant is not deployed
entry.content:
Hi!

This behavior is expected when working with Parallel Line workspaces in SAP Central Business Configuration.

When an organizational unit (for example, a Plant) is created in a Parallel Line, it is owned by that workspace. Even though the unit is visible in the organizational structure within CBC (in both Main Line and Parallel Line), it is not yet available in the Main Customizing Tenant.

This happens because organizational units are only deployed to the customizing tenant associated with their owning workspace and as a result, when an organizational unit is created in the Parallel Line, it is not automatically transferred to the Main Line customizing tenant.

You may see the organizational unit in both lines within CBC, but it will only be available in the corresponding customizing tenant depending on where it was created.

How to make it available in Main Line:

To make an organizational unit created in a Parallel Line available in the Main Customizing Tenant, you should follow this process:

  1. Complete and validate your configuration in the Parallel Line workspace
    • Ensure that all settings have been properly tested and confirmed before proceeding
  2. Execute a Merge into the Main Line
    • The Merge operation should only be performed once the changes in the Parallel Line are finalized
    • During the merge process, the system validates all changes and incorporates them into the Main Line
  3. Deploy the changes

After this process, the organizational unit will become available in the Main Customizing environment.

For additional information about Parallel Line and Merge, please see:

  • Merge - Overview | SAP Help Portal
  • Changes in Your Parallel Line | SAP Help Portal

Best Regards,

entry.title: Re: Country Compliance Forms "Notice of Pay Rate" California WTPA form Field Configuration

entry.title: Changing condition supplements doesn't create a worklist entry for automat document adjustment
entry.content:
I have set up automatich document adjustment. When I change the gross price in an info record, a worklist entry is created. If I change gross price and condition supplement (e.g. RA00), a worklist entry is created, too.

But if I only change the condition supplement, no worklist entry is created.

Is there any missing setting?

entry.title: Onboarding - Rehire with old Employment Error
entry.content:
Hello,

We are trying to rehire a former employee, Luis Alberto Ramirez, into position 90000546. The action being used is Rehire with Old Employment.

However, when attempting to submit the hiring action, we encounter the following screen with an error:

Error:

An application error has occurred. Please try again later or contact support if the issue persists (include the following error information:
errorId=67273211-5abd-43a1-8a18-f962fa5bf052,
timestamp=2026-04-06T20:37:24.026+0000,
along with the version information of the current screen).

Has anyone experienced a similar issue or can provide guidance on what might be causing this?

entry.title: Re: Add a custom field to the app "Stock - Multiple Materials" (F1595)
entry.content: Hey @ChrisH1993! Would you mind sharing which route you took that seemed to work for you? Was it the custom Z version of the app, using custom fields app, CDS view extensions, or modifying MPC_EXT and DPC_EXT classes? What version / year was your SAP system running on as well as Fiori version?

entry.title: Re: BI2025: Side-by-Side Migration vs. In-Place Update.
entry.content: I usually use a hybrid approach, where I install the current version on new servers, copy over the db and filestore, adjust configs for server specific settings, make sure that works the same as the live system, create a snapshot of the new environment, then run the upgrade on the new environment, then more testing.  Then on go live weekend, revert to the snapshot, copy over the db and filestore again, adjust configs for server specific settings, and run the upgrade on the new system again, then more testing.

entry.title: Re: CO-PC Change Work Center Cost Center (CR02)
entry.content:
Unlike FI objects, CO objects are time relevant and time sensitive. Therefore, it's important to utilize similar date period for all relevant Cost Objects in SAP CO.

  1. CR02 - You can plan to assign NEW cost center to work center by changing the period:
    1. All future postings will be assigned to new cost center
    2.  If you want to change already posted values to NEW cost center you can do that as well by changing document rules in FB02 + Account assignment reconfiguration in OKB9 + Reposting using OKBA or KB61.
  2. New cost calculation using NEW cost center - I believe you are referring to KP26 Cost Center/Activity Type rate change and calculation for Production Orders.
  3. For old cost centers for variance, and WIP calculation, you can apply the solution provided above to effect the change prospectively.

Regards, Pranay Sahay

entry.title: Re: Question: in "Manage Cost Centers" can the fields to...
entry.content:
1. There are 2 ways of managing this concern: Profile and Authorization method:
    1. User Responsible technical name is VERAK_USER
    2. Modify authorizations for object K_CCA in PFCG (Basis or GRC will have access)
    3. Update the Role in PFCG to set 'Activity' to display only (03) for specific cost centers or range (KOSTL)
  2. SAP Validation Rule GGB0/OB28: To restrict user from editing cost centers (transactions like KS02), create a validation rule in OB28 at Line Item level that triggers an error if SYST-UNAME = VERAK_USER. This validation checks user ID (SYST-UNAME) and forces an error message, preventing the save action.

    Regards, Pranay Sahay

entry.title: Cross App Navigation: Friendly Navigation in FLP - Quick Guide
entry.content:
Introduction:

This blog is going to help the people who just started the journey in SAPUI5/Fiori. It’s very basic level content which would help the beginners to understand Cross App Navigation and how it works.

In enterprise SAP applications, navigation isn’t just about moving between pages. it’s about maintaining consistency, security and scalability across multiple apps. That’s where the FLP friendly approach came into the picture.

If you are building apps with SAPUI5 inside the SAP Fiori Launchpad, relying on raw URLs or window.location is a shortcut that will eventually cause problems. Instead, SAP provides a structured service for cross-application navigation.

 

Overview:

Cross-App Navigation is a way for one app to open another app by describing what you want to do, not where it is. It also known as Intent-Based Navigation

One Liner: Cross-App Navigation = Navigate by intention, not by URL, with FLP resolving the correct app dynamically. 

As a quick analogy which will help you to understand easily and recall it wisely.

Imagine that you are going to a hospital for the first time, you wouldn’t usually say/ask that you have to go to building B, floor 3, room 27. Instead you would prefer to say,  I need to see a cardiologist. Then the hospital directs you correctly.

That’s exactly how navigation works in SAP using semantic intent. We have to describe what we have to do, not where it is.

                                                                                       Picture 1: SAP Fiori Navigation

 

Key Concepts

1. Semantic Object - Represents a business entity (e.g., Supplier, SalesOrder)

2. Action - Defines what you want to do (e.g., display, create, manage)

3. Parameters - Used to pass data between apps (like SupplierId)

 

How it works?

Let’s understand how it works. SAP recommends using the CrossApplicationNavigation service, which actually  works on semantic objects and actions.

                                                                                  Picture 2: SAPUI5 Intent based Navigation

 

Example Snippet:

Source App:
// In our Source or current app:
sap.ushell.Container.getService("CrossApplicationNavigation")
  .toExternal({
    target: {
      semanticObject: "SupplierQuotation",
      action: "display" // action can be like anything (display or manage)
    },
    params: {
      supplierId: "SUP123" // As usual Key value pair, Here SalesOrderId is Key Property & 12345 is the value
      isActive: false // Please convert this as Boolean value at target app
    }
  });

                                                                                 Snippet 1: Source App Code - To do Cross App

Target App:
// In our Target App: 
onInit: function () {
const oComponentData = this.getOwnerComponent().getComponentData();
const oParams = oComponentData?.startupParameters;
if (oParams && oParams.supplierId) {
const sSupId = oParams.supplierId?.[0]; // always array
const bIsActive = Boolean(isActive?.[0]);  // always array & we are converting to boolean using Boolean(X)
console.log("Supplier Id:", sSupId);
console.log("Is Active:", bIsActive ? “Yes” : “No”);
}
}

                                                                             Snippet 2: Target App Code - To handle parameters

This is how we can retrieve it and handle the data.

 

What Happens Behind the Scenes?

When you trigger navigation:

  • FLP resolves the intent (#SupplierQuotation)
  • Matches it to a configured app
  • Launches the correct application
  • Passes parameters securely 

 

URL Understanding: (Let’s Breakdown)

#SupplierQuotation-display?supplierId=SUP123&isActive=true

                                                                  Picture 3: SAPUI5 Intent based Navigation - URL Understanding

For your understanding:
URL      => #SupplierQuotation-display?supplierId=SUP123&isActive=true 
#        => means FLP Routing trigger
Supplier => means Semantic Object
display  => means Action
?        => means start of the parameters
supplierId=SUP123&isActive=true  => means Business Data

/*IMPORTANT*/
How it basically passes: {
supplierId: ["SUP123"],
isActive: ["true"]
}

                                                                          Snippet 3: URL Understanding Notes

Why Not Use Direct URLs?

We can navigate like this: window.location.href = "/sap/bc/ui5_ui5/sap/Z_SALES_APP/index.html?orderId=123";

But this approach:

  • Breaks in FLP environments
  • Ignores role-based navigation
  • Doesn’t integrate with intent-based routing
  • Becomes hard to maintain across landscapes

 

Benefits of FLP Navigation:

  •  Role-based access control
  •  Loose coupling between apps
  •  Centralized navigation configuration
  •  Works across systems (Dev, QA, Prod)
  •  Supports deep linking & bookmarks

 

Challenges of FLP Navigation:

While handling SAPUI5 navigation parameter, we have to keep in our mind, that values are always  wrapped as arrays, String representation even though  it got passed as Number or Boolean and so we have to convert it properly in the target app.

                                                                 Picture 4: SAPUI5 Navigation Parameter Handling Challenges

 

When Should You Use This?

Use FLP navigation when:

  • Moving between SAP Fiori apps
  • Working inside Launchpad
  • Building enterprise-grade solutions

Avoid it only if you’re building a standalone UI5 app outside FLP

 

Conclusion:

Overall, cross app navigation in FLP separates intent from implementation, allowing applications to remain loosely coupled, easier to maintain and aligned with enterprise-level standards.

Hope that you got some idea about Cross app navigation in SAPUI5 in FLP Intent way.

Thank you for taking the time to read this blog! If you found this helpful, i would love to hear your thoughts, feedback or questions in the comments. Let's keep learning and growing together!

I’m still new to blogging, so if you notice anything that could be improved or corrected, please don’t hesitate to let me know!!

entry.title: Re: What I Wish I Knew
entry.content: One of the first things I wish I had understood better is that SAP doesn’t solve process problems. It exposes them. When the process is well-defined, the system works consistently and reliably. When it isn’t, it merely makes the flaws more visible—and often harder to fix. That’s why investing time in understanding the process, talking to the business, and asking “why” always yields better results than jumping straight into configuration or development.

entry.title: Re: General description of SAP Activate methodology
entry.content: The go.support.sap.com link is no longer valid. The new link is https://me.sap.com/roadmapviewer

entry.title: Re: Onboarding - Rehire with old Employment Error
entry.content:
I have seen similar behavior earlier. This type of error usually comes from backend processing during Rehire with Old Employment, not from a direct configuration issue.

In most cases, it’s due to inconsistencies in old employment data. I would suggest checking the employee’s Job Information history / Job Info records — especially event/event reason, mandatory fields, and effective dating.

Also try rehiring with a different position to rule out any position-specific issue with 90000546.

If it still fails, the errorId and timestamp will be required for SAP to trace it from backend logs.

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: Easier access to SAP methodologies
entry.content: The link to roadmap viewer https://go.support.sap.com/roadmapviewer/# has been replaced with Roadmap Viewer - SAP for Me.

entry.title: Re: Easier access to SAP methodologies
entry.content: The link to roadmap viewer https://go.support.sap.com/roadmapviewer/# has been replaced with Roadmap Viewer - SAP for Me.

entry.title: Re: yaai & yaai_ui – Open-Source AI Tools for ABAP Developers
entry.content:
What a library!! Thanks for doing this!!
I installed the YAAI, YAAI_UI, YAAI_FC, and YAAI_COCKPIT.
I managed to get the getting started app working with hard coding the OPENAI key in the code. However, I can't find how to configure like it says I should in the sample YAAI_UI_R_QUICKSTART_CHAT code example:

"This example assumes that the API base URL and the API Key are properly configured"

How do I configure this? I can't find it in the documentation.

Also, when I run the YAAI_COCKPIT I get a pop-up in my browser asking for a username and password everywhere I click. My backend credentials do not seem to work. What am I missing?

Thanks again.

David

entry.title: Re: Eligible Users with Multiple Employments not appearin...
entry.content:
Hi  @RyanPerkins 

Could you test with another user on multiple employments that shows the active record first when you look them up ? You would need to add employee history for that person to make a valid case before retesting.

Or try fixing that test employee 725892 with the below steps and retry.

Xavier

--- 

Troubleshooting: EC Multiple Employments Default Profile Issue

Problem Description

When employees have multiple employments in Employee Central, the system defaults to displaying the inactive profile instead of the active employment profile. This affects user experience and access to current employment data.

Root Cause Analysis

The employment switcher functionality in Employee Central has specific system logic for determining which profile displays by default. When users sign in, the system automatically selects the default employment (Home Assignment or Primary Employment) as the current active employment . However, there are known limitations with the employment switcher feature that can cause it to default to inactive profiles.

The Employment Switcher is available for users with multiple employments, but this feature is not yet fully functional. There is an ongoing enhancement being tracked in ECT-114530 that addresses features across both EC and Platform modules .

Solution Steps

  1. Navigate to Company System and Logo Settings
    • Go to Admin Center → Company System and Logo Settings
    • Look for employment-related display settings
    • Configure Employment Display Settings
      • Enable "Show all active employments (for global assignment and concurrent employment) on quick card, org chart, and global search results" 
      • This setting controls visibility of multiple employments across the system
      • Check Mobile App Settings
        • For mobile users, navigate to More → Settings (Android: menu icon → Settings)
        • Use the "Current Employment" setting to switch between active employments 
        • Select the desired employment and tap "Switch" to make it the current active employment
        • Verify Permission Settings
          • Ensure users have proper role-based permissions to view multiple employments
          • Check that "Include Inactive Employees in the search" permission is configured appropriately

            Configuration Details

            • Required Fields: Employment switcher requires proper configuration of User ID, Username, and Person ID External identifiers 
            • Permission Roles: Users need General User Permission → Company Info Access and User Search permission for specified target populations 
            • Field-Level Permissions: Access to Job Information → Job Title and Location fields is required for employment information display

              Validation Steps

              1. Test with Sample Employee Record
                • Log in as a user with multiple employments
                • Verify that the active employment displays by default
                • Test the employment switcher functionality
                • Verify Active Profile Display
                  • Check that employment status shows as "Active" in the default view
                  • Confirm that inactive employments are properly labeled and accessible through the switcher
                  • Check System-Wide Impact
                    • Test in People Profile, Org Chart, and search results
                    • Verify mobile app behavior matches web application settings

                      Additional Considerations

                      • Known Limitations: The employment switcher has ongoing functional limitations tracked in enhancement ECT-114530 [2]
                      • Future Enhancement: Engineering is working on improvements to the employment switcher functionality 
                      • Impact on Talent Modules: Performance forms and other talent processes are based on employment (User ID), so proper employment selection affects access to these features [5]
                      • HRIS Sync Considerations: Be aware that HRIS Sync job behavior can affect employment status, particularly with case-sensitive User ID scenarios 

                        Note: If the issue persists after configuration changes, monitor enhancement ECT-114530 for updates on when the employment switcher functionality will be fully resolved.

@RyanPerkins

entry.title: Re: Group revenue GL from SD billing in public cloud
entry.content:
Hello!

 

It is not possible to force SD billing to post only one summarized revenue line in Finance when a billing document contains multiple line items. This behavior is by design.

In Public Cloud, SAP follows an item‑level posting principle:

  • Each billing item creates its own accounting impact
  • Revenue lines are split whenever there is a difference in:  Revenue G/L account, WBS element, Profit Center, Tax code, other account‑assignment characteristics

 Although cannot fully summarize revenue into one line in FI, is the options of aggregation in reporting. If the requirement is mainly how the data is viewed, rather than how it is posted:

  • Financial postings remain detailed and compliant
  • Reporting can show one revenue amount grouped by G/L and WBS

Is not possible posting all SD billing revenue into one single FI line.

entry.title: Agenda - SAP Inside Track Recife 2026
entry.content:
O SIT Recife 2026 promete ser um dia incrível, com uma agenda totalmente conectada às tendências mais atuais do SAP, como S/4HANA 2025, IA e transformação digital. Desde a abertura celebrando os 10 anos da SAP Community Recife até o painel final, o evento traz conteúdos práticos e estratégicos, passando por temas como Business AI, Clean Core, jornada Cloud e o futuro do consultor SAP. Além das palestras com especialistas de empresas como SAP, Capgemini e Accenture, teremos ótimos momentos de networking ao longo do dia e no happy hour. Será uma experiência completa, dinâmica e indispensável para quem quer evoluir e se manter relevante no ecossistema SAP.

 Agenda e cronograma do evento:

HorárioAtividadeResponsávelEmpresa
09:00 – 09:20Abertura oficial + Premiação 10 anos SAP Community RecifeMarssel Vilaça / OrganizaçãoSAP Champion
09:20 – 09:50Por que decidimos ir para o S/4HANA 2025? Desafios da atualização e uso de IA com JouleAntonio FigueredoGrupo Cornélio Brennand
10:00 – 10:30A conectividade em SAP Finanças e sua contribuição para a digitalizaçãoManuel RobalinhoSAP Champion
10:30 – 11:00☕ Coffee Break (horário nobre networking)Hotel / OrganizaçãoKastel Manibu
11:00 – 11:30Quem vai matar sua Z: Clean Core ou a IA?Leandro DanteLab2Learn
11:40 – 12:10Business AI começa no funcional: Fundamentos e Desafios da Adoção RealThiago Verta EirasCapgemini
12:10 – 13:30🍽️ AlmoçoOrganização—
13:30 – 13:50🎁 Sorteio de brindes + chamada para retorno pós-almoçoOrganizaçãoParceiros
13:50 – 14:20Consultor SAP: Extinção ou Evolução?Leandro BarbosaFusion Consultoria
14:30 – 15:00Jornada da manutenção digital no SAPLuciano Martins RodriguesAccenture
15:10 – 15:40Como o Suporte SAP apoia sua Jornada Cloud!Edson ThomasSAP
15:40 – 16:10☕ Coffee Break (tarde)Hotel / OrganizaçãoKastel Manibu
16:10 – 16:40O Fim do Fiori tradicional: IA, Agentes e Low-Code Reinventando o Desenvolvimento SAPGabriel AndradeNeptune
16:50 – 17:20Palestra Capgemini 2 – Como utilizar o SAP Signavio para resolver o gap de conhecimento nos processos de negócioRogério ToscanoCapgemini
17:20 – 17:50Painel Final: Tendências SAPTBDDelloite
17:50 – 18:00🎁 Sorteio final + Encerramento oficialOrganizaçãoParceiros
18:00 – 20:00🍻 Happy Hour / NetworkingOrganização—

entry.title: SAP GTS - Legal Changes Hot info - April Edition
entry.content:
NL DMS:  Digitaal Dossier for document submission in the Netherlands

The Regulatory change manager (RCM) has been updated with information on the Digital Dossier legal change HERE

The planned delivery date is 31 Aug. 2026, as outlined in the announcement: 

  • The Digitaal Dossier is a new Dutch Customs initiative that modernizes how companies submit documents for import and export declarations.
  • The legal change is in a transition phase. The new standard procedure is expected to be established by May 16, 2026. A potential transitional period is being further developed. Customs by default expects companies to arrange the necessary connection at the starting date of the Digitaal Dossier functionality by May 16th. As the delivery of this legal change is not planned at this point but during the transitional phase, please inform Dutch Customs no later than May 1st by sending an E-Mail to Douane.Digitaal.Dossier@douane.nl.
    Consequently the published due date is an estimate until final timeline is communicated by customs authorities.

Given this information, if relevant for your business, please contact asap the Douane email as above,  to state that the the May 16th date cannot be met by your company, as not informing Customs may lead to further issues. 

 

PASSAR NE015 v5:  With increment DFEA-4210, Swiss customs introduced version 5 of the export declaration (NE015). The new message version includes enhancements for warehouse traffic, partial deliveries, and other changes. A migration to version 5 is absolutely necessary to continue to enable the message exchange.

A number of notes are currently available around the changes.
Please refer to note:
3720167 - CH Passar export: Changeover and adjustments for version 5 of export declaration (NE015 v5)

Additional Notes: 

3740133CH Passar Export: Incompleteness check for version 5 of export declaration (NE015 v5) - part 2
3624365FAQ and corrections for Passar 1.0 export
3720168CH Passar Export: Incompleteness check for version 5 of export declaration (NE015 v5)
3713392CH Passar Export: XML Definition and XML Mapping for Version 5 of Export Declaration (NE015 v5)

Please note:Notes related to the change of decimal places for weights and quantities are in process and will be released in due course. (as of 27.04.26)
3744948 CH Passar Ausfuhr: XML-Mapping für Gewichte und Mengen mit 3-Nachkommastellen (NE015 v5)
3744949  CH Passar Ausfuhr: Datenbeschaffung für Gewichte und Mengen mit 3 Nachkommastellen (NE015 v5) 
3744950  CH Passar Ausfuhr: Unvollständigkeitsprüfung für Gewichte und Mengen mit 3-Nachkommastellen (NE015 v5)

entry.title: Re: Advanced FCV unrealized posting remains open after pa...
entry.content:
Hello!

 

Key principle behind Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation

  • Unrealized FX differences are created, updated, and reversed only by the valuation run
  • Operational postings (invoice clearing, payment postings) do not touch valuation postings
  • Valuation postings are temporary accounting adjustments valid as of a specific key date, not permanent changes to the transactional document chain

 

What exactly happens in presented scenario (step by step):

Step 1 – Supplier invoice

  • Invoice posted: 1,000 USD
  • Vendor open item carries:
  • • Transaction currency: USD
  • • Local currency translated at invoice exchange rate

No valuation impact yet.

 

Step 2 – Period-end Foreign Currency Valuation run

  • Valuation key date: e.g. 31.03
  • Exchange rate difference as loss
  • System posts:
  • • 100 EUR unrealized FX loss
  • • Counter-account: FX valuation adjustment account
  • Vendor item is valuation-adjusted, but:
  • • Original invoice document is untouched
  • • Valuation is stored as a separate valuation layer

 

Step 3 – Payment is posted (before next valuation)

When the payment is posted:

  • The system calculates realized FX difference vs. the original invoice amount
  • It ignores valuation adjustments
  • Result:
  • • A realized FX difference at clearing
  • • The valuation posting (100 EUR) remains open and posted

This is because:

Foreign Currency Valuation adjustments are not part of the clearing logic, are not transactional corrections

 

Step 4 – Next valuation run

  • System detects:
  • • Original open item no longer exists
  • Therefore:
  • • The previous unrealized valuation (100 EUR) is automatically reversed
  • Net effect over time:
  • • Unrealized FX → reversed
  • • Realized FX remains

Accounting result is correct over the full lifecycle

 

SAP does not reverse valuation at payment posting because:

  1. Valuation belongs to a key date

Unrealized FX differences are only valid as of a reporting date (e.g. period end).

If SAP reversed valuation at clearing time:

  • It would mix operational events with reporting adjustments
  • This would break auditability and valuation traceability

 

  1. SAP separates:
  • Operational accounting (invoices, payments, clearing)
  • Reporting adjustments (valuation, impairment, accruals)

Only the valuation engine is allowed to:

  • Create
  • Update
  • Reverse valuation postings

 

  1. Ledger and parallel accounting consistency

In S/4HANA Cloud:  Foreign Currency Valuation often runs per ledger and invoices are not Ledger specific

 

From an accounting point of view:

  •  Financial statements at each closing date are correct
  •  Intra-period balances may look counter-intuitive between:
  • Payment date and
  • Next valuation run

you temporarily see:

  • A valuation posting without an open foreign currency vendor item because:
  • Unrealized FX is assessed only at reporting dates
  • No requirement exists to clear valuation immediately when clearing occurs

 

There is also the option to run Foreign Currency Valuation more often than once per month if is needed.

entry.title: Re: Week 4: AI Developer Challenge SAP-RPT-1 Model
entry.content:
User ID: GE311613

Thank you for the challenge! To get the correct predictions, I modified the following fields in the CSV:

  1. Changed the SALESGROUP for "Desktop Computer" and "Macbook" from "Enterprise Solutions" to "Consumer Electronics".

After these changes, the model correctly predicted:

  • Laptop -> Consumer Electronics

  • Office Chair -> Office Furniture

entry.title: Re: Week 3 - Use AI Agents with Grounding Tools
entry.content: Week 3 completed! UserId:  GE306937

entry.title: Re: Live Session: Get Certified - SAP Generative AI Developer | New Dates Added – Register Now!

Join Sidharth Gupta on May 6th, 13th, and 20th for interactive sessions covering essential topics, practical demonstrations, and exam strategies. Whether you’re a beginner or experienced developer, these sessions will guide you through SAP’s Generative AI tools, hands-on exercises, and certification prep. Don’t miss this opportunity to boost your skills and get ready for the C_AIG certification. Secure your spot today and take the next step in your SAP career! Register now for the May sessions!

entry.title: Re: 3XK In-House Repair – Spare Parts Consumption Timing...
entry.content: Hi Chris, Thanks a lot for the detailed explanation — really helpful and confirms our understanding of the 3XK behaviour. We’ll do some further testing with competing demands (e.g. sales orders) to see how reservation and ATP behave in practice. Based on that, we’ll assess whether the standard process is sufficient or if we need to consider a small extension for better control between warehouse and service. Appreciate your help! Best regards, Wei

entry.title: Unlock the Power of Artificial Intelligence with SAP Learning: Courses, Certifications & More
entry.content:
Artificial Intelligence (AI) is transforming the way businesses operate, making it crucial to stay up-to-date with the latest advancements and best practices. SAP Learning is at the forefront of this revolution, offering a comprehensive suite of training opportunities tailored to boost your expertise and career in AI.

Featured Learning on SAP: Joule

Meet Joule—SAP’s intelligent AI assistant designed to accelerate business transformation. Whether you’re new to Joule or aiming to integrate AI-driven agents throughout your organizational system landscape, SAP’s curated courses will help you harness its full potential:

  • Introducing Joule: Get started with the basics and discover how Joule is reshaping intelligent business processes.
  • Boosting AI-driven Business Transformation with Joule Agents: Learn to deploy AI agents that unlock new efficiencies and innovation.
  • Implementing Joule Across Your Organizational System Landscape: Dive into a guided journey for seamless integration across your enterprise.

AI Foundation: Build, Solve, and Certify

Ready to put generative AI to work? SAP offers hands-on training and globally recognized certifications:

  • Solve Your Business Problems Using Prompts and LLMs in SAP Generative AI Hub: Leverage large language models (LLMs) and intelligent prompts to address real-world challenges.
  • SAP Certified – SAP Generative AI Developer: Validate your expertise and showcase your skill set with this in-demand certification, tailored for today’s data-driven environment.

Explore More: Expert-Led Sessions & Learning Journeys

SAP Learning goes beyond traditional courses. Benefit from expert-led live sessions and immersive, guided learning paths that ensure you not only gain knowledge but also practical, real-world skills. Our Artificial Intelligence Featured Learning Page is your one-stop shop to discover the full spectrum of AI offerings.

Why Choose SAP Learning for AI?

  • Skill-building for the future: Acquire knowledge that aligns with the latest business needs and technological breakthroughs.
  • Expert guidance: Learn from top professionals and AI practitioners.
  • Certification: Stand out with credentials that demonstrate your AI expertise to employers and peers.

Take the next step in your AI journey with SAP Learning.
Master artificial intelligence, get certified, and shape the future of business innovation. Visit the Artificial Intelligence Featured Learning Page to get started today!

entry.title: Re: Hire Template Configuration: Supported MDF Object Types and Implementation Guidelines for Onboar
entry.content:
I don’t understand the reasoning behind this limitation.
One of the key selling points of Onboarding 2.0 at launch was that it would remain on the same framework as Employee Central, so this seems inconsistent with that expectation.

entry.title: Re: Mass Sales Order Creation via Excel Upload – Handling...
entry.content: faced the same challenge building a similar solution. The key insight: separate the data preparation layer (Excel → mapped fields) from the SAP upload step. Happy to share what worked for field mapping and required vs optional fields if useful.

Únete a nuestra sesión de Gestión del Cambio Continuo y aprende estrategias clave para garantizar transiciones fluidas y maximizar el impacto de tus proyectos en SAP SuccessFactors. Descubre cómo integrar la gestión del cambio, involucrar a los equipos y medir resultados para fortalecer la resiliencia organizacional. ¡Una sesión ideal para líderes y profesionales enfocados en el éxito del cambio continuo!

¡No te lo pierdas!

entry.title: Re: Compatibility Pack: How to check it in your system going forward?
entry.content: @herbert_strobl251  you can download Simplification Item Catalog from https://me.sap.com/sic and use "Upload Simplification Item Catalog from file" buttor to update the data.

entry.title: Re: Batch Creation not happening in app F1796 App
entry.content: Hi SAP Public Cloud Support Team, pls help with this.

entry.title: How to implement Data Privacy and Retention Architecture in Success Factors
entry.content:
Hi Community 

I would like to understand  how data retention rules are configured in your organisations. 

What configuration options are available within SuccessFactors to support retention and deletion
Any constraints or risks we should be aware of
Recommended approach and next steps to move this into delivery

Thanks

entry.title: Re: How has RISE with SAP changed ownership across teams...
entry.content:
Customers that are already under SAP RISE, I'd say it's an operational model different for Basis people, as per SAP RISE roles and responsibilities

SAP is in charge of managing the lower level of an SAP System, such as infra, OS, Networking, OS patching, etc., but also at some higher level, such as access to the 000, which is limited to SAP and activities that only SAP can do.

From an operational perspective, the customer will require a partner or internal staff to more strategically and be the bridge to translate Business Requirements to Technical ones when it is required, such as:

  • Coordinating maintenance windows
  • Opening requests to the SAP RISE team to request something that only SAP can do
  • Proper planning for the request between the organization and SAP RISE support to achieve the desired outcome.

I would say it is not simple because of this operational model itself, but it is more strategic and different.

Shifting from tradditional operational model to this SAP RISE is something to embrace and pushes us to have a new mindset.

entry.title: Validation Error: Exception Subprocess should start with Error Start Event and end with End Message
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently working on an Integration Flow,I’m running into a persistent validation error that is preventing a clean deployment.

The Error Message:

"Exception Subprocess should start with an Error Start Event and end with either End Message or Error End".

 

 

Any insights or best practices on how to resolve these validation flags would be greatly appreciated!

Best regards,

entry.title: BTP User Cannot Receive “Forgot Password” Email After Being Added in Subaccount
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am encountering an issue related to user creation and password reset in SAP BTP.

Scenario:
- I created a user in a BTP subaccount via Security → Users.
- The user was successfully created and assigned roles.

Issue:
- When the user tries to log in and clicks "Forgot Password", no password reset email is received.
- The email address is correct and has been verified.
- The Spam/Junk folder has also been checked.

Important observation:
- My own email address was not created in the IAS tenant, but I was still able to receive emails.

Question:
- What configuration is required to enable password reset emails to be sent successfully?

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: cross-company STO VL02N Purchase order XXXXXX does not contain items for a stock transfer
entry.content:
In a cross-company STO scenario:

1. I created an NB-type stock transfer purchase order via ME21N, maintaining all required data including shipping details, supplying plant, and receiving plant.

2. I then created an NLCC-type delivery document via VL10B, which completed without any errors.

3. When attempting to post goods issue (PGI) via VL02N using movement type 643 (two-step cross-company transfer), the system throws error M7153: "Purchase order XXXXXX does not contain items for a stock transfer."

Could you please help me resolve this issue?  Thank you very much.

entry.title: Re: SF Learning: Online Session timeout
entry.content:
Hello Ramya,

In the application we already have a setting that was introduced to help customers who had issues when their users were taking long online courses. If a user takes a long online course, the BizX session could time out and prevent the completion from being communicated to the Learning.

This Learning setting (sessionMgmtNotifyModuleActivity) prevents the BizX session from timing out while a user is running an online course in Learning.

Please Follow the below steps:

  1. Please navigate to System Administration > Configuration > System Configuration > BizX >
  2. Search for the property sessionMgmtNotifyModuleActivity=false and check that if it is set to false. If the property is set to false, then only the browser based ping to BizX is used 
  3. Set it to true to ensure that Session Management service ping is used for keep alive of BizX session. With this setting, Learning is pinging BizX every 5 minutes in order to keep the session alive.

Important Checks On Customer Side: if you are still having issues navigating through Learning Management System(LMS) and content after checking the above setting, it may be getting blocked your side. Ensure your internal IT checks that your Internal Proxies are not blocking any connections between Learning Management System(LMS)and BizX.

With regards to adding a warning, you can probably look at the below idea that was already submitted & is currently under review status.

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/263029

entry.title: Wrong Data on Invoice
entry.content:
Hey guys, 

I require a change for the invoice address on the invoice with number CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER. Invoice is already paid. 

I got the invoice from me.sap.com --> Finance & Legal --> Billing --> Open Invoices

I cannot get invoice specific assistance as the button for it is greyed out. 

Please mail me the correct invoice. 
Thanks in advance!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Wrong Data on Invoice
entry.content:
Dear customer,

If you need to change the invoice address on the invoice, and you cannot click 'Get Assistance' button in the Invoices card because the button is grey-out,

then could you pls kindly refer to KBA 3107749 and contact the SAP Cash Collection team by sending email to FinanceAR@sap.com ?

In the email, provide your invoice/billing questions, your customer number/name and full details and screenshots of the invoice/invoice number. 

Thanks and best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Build Process Automation Capturing Element in Automation
entry.content:
Hi all,

I am trying to capture the Green Tick in STMS_IMPORT that shows that the TR is Imported Successfully but i am not able to do that. Does anyone know how to use RPA to know that the TR is imported or not would love some advice cause that icon is not giving me anything nor code nothing how can i capture that icon.

SAP Build

entry.title: HANA Cockpit retention not deleting old SYSTEMDB backup generations although log_mode is normal
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am testing HANA Cockpit backup retention for SYSTEMDB@HSD and would like to clarify if the behavior I am seeing is expected.

Configuration:

  • Backup destination: File
  • Backup path: /hana/shared/HSD/HDB00/backup/data/SYSTEMDB/
  • Daily complete data backup: around 02:00 AM
  • Automatically Delete Backup Generations: Yes
  • Deletion option: Remove from backup catalog and delete physically from file system
  • Retain Backup Generations Younger Than: 1 day
  • Minimum No. of Retained Backup Generations: 1
  • Start Daily Automatic Deletion: 9:00 PM UTC
  • Assigned Database User: SYSTEM
  • log_mode = normal

On the morning of Apr 28, I still saw these complete data backup generations in both the backup catalog and file system:

2026-04-26 02:00
2026-04-27 02:00
2026-04-28 02:00

However, when I checked again on Apr 29, the Apr 26 backup generation had been deleted, and the remaining backup files were:

2026-04-27 02:00
2026-04-28 02:00
2026-04-29 02:00

So the retention policy seems to be working, but not exactly as I initially expected.

My questions are:

  1. Is this behavior expected for Retain Backup Generations Younger Than = 1 day?
  2. Does HANA Cockpit retention evaluate and delete old backup generations only once per daily scheduler run, instead of deleting them immediately when they become older than 1 day?
  3. Why would the Apr 26 generation still exist on Apr 28 but be deleted on Apr 29?
  4. Is the correct interpretation that HANA deletes complete backup generations based on the scheduler run and generation eligibility, not simply based on the file date?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: SAP Help needed on below items
entry.content: We helped you to accept the answer, since there were no updates after 7 days. You can unaccept it anytime if you have further questions.

entry.title: Re: Budget Design Process for Salary Increases: a functional overview
entry.content: I would like to know how to configure the Current Salary to be calculated and displayed as a prorated amount. Currently, the Current Salary in the Budget Assignment page shows the employee’s full-year salary. However, for employees who join mid-year or do not start on January 1st, the salary should be prorated based on their length of service. How can this be configured so that the Compensation Admin can allocate the budget accurately for each team head?

entry.title: Default Billing Settings in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi all,

i want to know is there any step to make billing settings apply to all user ? i have a conditions that when we create billing, we can set different date between billing plan and actual billing and i want to make it already as the setting when we go live.

if there any step to make default billing settings to all user, please let know how to maintain it.

 

thankyou

entry.title: Sending billing documents to multiple email addresses if customer and payer are different
entry.content:
Hi all,

We have some invoices where the payer is different to the customer. Currently, these are emailed to the payer, but we would like to also CC the customer's email address.

I understand it is possible to set up additional email recipients through Output Parameter Determination, but I haven't been able to find any information about how to do this specific thing. Is anyone able to please provide any advice?

We are using S4Hana Cloud Public.

Thanks

entry.title: Request new S-user but no enrollment email sent to the user
entry.content:
Requested new S-user.

This user is part of my team. I had raised a request for him to have a login. But he did not get the enrollment mail, I subsequently deleted his entry so that I could recreate it new. His user is still lying in pending for creation.

Could you please expedite the process?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Request new S-user but no enrollment email sent to th...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

If new S-user has been created successfully but user didn't get the welcome email to activate the new S-user, then could you pls kindly refer to the following KBA:

  • KBA 2490845 - New S-user ID did not receive a Welcome e-mail to set their password - SAP for Me

and access the URL https://accounts.sap.com/ui/createForgottenPasswordMail?spId=55365985e4b07dc3abdfc16c&targetUrl=&sourceUrl= to set the new password with the 'Forgot my password' feature?

Hope this works.

Thanks and regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Basic and extended document splitting in SAP S/4 Hana
entry.content:
We have been trying to add the new custom fields for GL document splitting, but the fields are not available to add in the config. node - 'Define Document Splitting Characteristics for General Ledger Accounting'.

The custom fields were created through CFL and are already available in ACDOCA, but for some reason not available to add in the config. node 'Define Document Splitting Characteristics for General Ledger Accounting'.

Can you pls suggest if ACDOCA custom fields can be added as Doc Splitting characteristics.

entry.title: Re: Explanation Document Splitting with Configuration Steps & Various Real Time Scenarios!
entry.content:
We have been trying to add the new custom fields for GL document splitting, but the fields are not available to add in the config. node - 'Define Document Splitting Characteristics for General Ledger Accounting'.

The custom fields were created through CFL and are already available in ACDOCA, but for some reason not available to add in the config. node 'Define Document Splitting Characteristics for General Ledger Accounting'.

Can you pls suggest if ACDOCA custom fields can be added as Doc Splitting characteristics.

entry.title: Question Regarding Authorization Requirement for Finance Plan Import (BUKRS_GLRLDNR_CATEGORY_P)
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We encountered the following authorization error when the customer attempted to import finance plan data:

Error Message: FINS_ACDOCP_PLANNING056
You do not have authorization to save planning data for source xx, category Z_FI_TAG00, controlling area Axxx, company code 00xx.
Please maintain authorization object K_ACDOCP_P.

As a workaround, we resolved the issue by changing the restriction of the customer's business role "BR_CORP_ADV" for:

Company Code / Ledger / Category (BUKRS_GLRLDNR_CATEGORY_P)
→ Set the entire restriction to Unrestricted (*) for all values

After setting the restriction type to unrestricted for all Company Code / Ledger / Category values, the finance plan import worked successfully.

Question

Does anyone know since when this unrestricted setting for all values of BUKRS_GLRLDNR_CATEGORY_P became required in order to import finance plan data?

Specifically:

  • Was this requirement introduced/changed in a particular version upgrade?
  • Or has this been standard SAP behavior for a long time?

We found a similar KBA dated 2025/11/18, so it appears this may not be related to 2602VUP.
However, we would like to understand when/why this authorization setup became necessary.

Any insight would be appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: HANA Cockpit retention not deleting old SYSTEMDB back...
entry.content:
Hi @FPT ,

Thank you for this very well structured question summary.

I think the HANA Cockpit is functioning as expected.
Please note that the "backup generations" in HANA Cockpit differ from the number of data backup files.
Please refer to SAP Note 2852302 - Backup Retention Policy is not Working as Expected, which provides detailed information on how the retention policy works.
You can find the note here: https://me.sap.com/notes/2852302.

Additional remark, if you check the data backups between April 29, 21:00 PM and April 30, 02:00 AM, I think you will likely find only two data backups remaining:
---------------------------------------
2026-04-28 02:00
2026-04-29 02:00
---------------------------------------

Best regards,
Charles

entry.title: Adding custom columns to the capacity planning ALV in sap EPPM.
entry.content:
Hello,

How to add custom columns to the capacity planning ALV in sap EPPM.

I could find some info on adding columns to financial planning ALV in KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3253242 .

 

Thanks,

Suhas

 

SAP Portfolio and Project Management

entry.title: Change Management - Steps to help drive Adoption and Value Realisation from SuccessFactors HCM
entry.content:
Overview

Creating a successful change management strategy requires addressing key business considerations and decisions that align all stakeholders with a unified vision. By collaborating on this process, you can develop your initial program documentation while identifying any gaps and areas needing further exploration.

 

Join us for an interactive webinar where Transformation Advisor Nicole Wright will share real-world examples and leading practices from various industries. This is a unique opportunity to engage with peers, ask questions, and learn from the experiences of professionals across different organizations

 

Who Should Attend

HR and IT Business Leads

Project Sponsors

Change Manager

Project Manager

Super Administrators

HR and IT Functional Leads

SME

 

We will talk about

Program Design

Persona Development 

Communication 

Training Planning 

Sustainment & Reinforcement

SAP Change Management resources

Output Parameter Determination | SAP Help Portal

Regards,

Anand Raichura
